prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. In line with the 2018 changes, municipal elections in Israel will take place on October 31, 2023, and election day will be a paid day off. All municipalities, local councils, and regional councils will be up for election, as well as their leaders. Top-two runoffs will be held on 14 November in all localities where no leadership candidate won 40% or more of the vote in the first round. Voters in municipalities and local councils will choose their leader and council in two separate ballots that are colored yellow and white respectively.
B. On October 31, 2023, Israel is scheduled to conduct its municipal elections. As a result of amendments introduced in 2018, the day of the election will be a remunerated holiday from work. Every municipality, local council, and regional council, along with their respective leaders, will be subject to election. For any locality where no leadership contender secured at least 40% of the vote during the initial round, a second round between the top two candidates will take place on November 14. Citizens residing in municipalities and local councils will select their leader and council through two distinct ballots, which will be yellow and white in color, respectively. Furthermore, voters within regional councils will cast their votes for a local committee utilizing blue-colored ballots. In instances where borough committees are established, blue ballots will also be employed for these committees. All elected officials will assume their positions following the completion of these electoral processes.
C. Municipal elections are set to be held in Israel on 31 October 2023. In accordance with changes made in 2018, election day will be a paid day off work. All municipalities, local councils and regional councils will be up for election, as will their leaders. Top-two runoffs will be held on 14 November in all localities where no leadership candidate won 40% or more of the vote in the first round.Voters in municipalities and local councils will pick their leader and council in two separate ballots that will be colored yellow and white respectively. Voters in regional councils will also vote for a local committee using ballots that are colored blue. blue ballots will also be used for borough committees in cases in which they exist. All elected

Answer:

Top-two runoffs will be held on 14 November in all localities where no leadership candidate won 40% or more of the vote in the first round. Voters in municipalities and local councils will choose their leader and council in two separate ballots that are colored yellow and white respectively.
options.B: On October 31, 2023, Israel is scheduled to conduct its municipal elections. As a result of amendments introduced in 2018, the day of the election will be a remunerated holiday from work. Citizens residing in municipalities and local councils will select their leader and council through two distinct ballots, which will be yellow and white in color, respectively. Furthermore, voters within regional councils will cast their votes for a local committee utilizing blue-colored ballots. In instances where borough committees are established, blue ballots will also be employed for these committees. All elected officials will assume their positions following the completion of these electoral processes.
